source_file: CVS/89.1 ACS - intro, PG, clinical features, diagnosis .md
title: ACUTE CORONARY SYNDROME

cards.content:
**Core concept:** ACS divides by ECG into STEMI and non-ST-elevation (NSTEMI/unstable angina). MI defined by troponin >99th percentile plus ischemia. Universal definition specifies Type 1 (plaque rupture) vs Type 2 (O2 mismatch, no angio needed).

**Key parameters:** Troponin men >26 ng/L, women >16 ng/L. Type 2 commonest non-obstructive. Q-wave classification obsolete.

**Clinical application:** Guides need for urgent reperfusion (STEMI) vs conservative/angiographic strategy (NSTEMI). Type 2 requires treating supply-demand cause.

**Interventions:** Type 1: reperfusion. Type 2: correct anemia/sepsis/tachyarrhythmia. No routine coronary angio.

**Pitfalls:** Assuming all MI need PCI; Type 2 mismatched O2 often non-obstructive. Troponin alone insufficient without ischemic symptom/ECG.

**Core concept:** STEMI mandates immediate reperfusion when ST elevation at J point in ≥2 contiguous leads meets sex/age thresholds without LVH/LBBB.

**Key parameters:** Men <40: ≥2.5mm; ≥40: ≥2mm. Women V2-V5: ≥1.5mm; other: ≥1mm. ECG within 10 min of contact. Add V7-V9 (posterior), V3R-V5R (RV) if inconclusive.

**Clinical application:** Identifies occluded artery territory; directs cath lab activation. Inferior + RV needs right leads to avoid misdiagnosis.

**Interventions:** Immediate PCI/fibrinolysis. Posterior: posterior leads. RV: right precordial leads.

**Pitfalls:** Missing posterior/RV MI with standard 12-lead. LBBB/new LBBB obscures ST; use Sgarbossa. PE mimics with RBBB.

**Core concept:** New LBBB or paced rhythm with ischemic symptoms requires PCI if high-risk features present; Sgarbossa score identifies MI on disguised ECG.

**Key parameters:** Sgarbossa ≥3 pts (conc ST↑ 1mm +QRS 5pt; conc ST↓ V1-3 3pt; discord ST↑≥5mm 2pt). Specificity 98%, sens 20%. PCI if shock, Sgarbossa≥3, or anterior akinesia.

**Clinical application:** Decides immediate cath vs wait enzymes when prior ECG unavailable. Paced = LBBB pattern, apply same.

**Interventions:** Meets criteria → immediate PCI. Unstable → cath anyway.

**Pitfalls:** RBBB not infarct sign; consider PE. Waiting enzymes in new LBBB with instability delays therapy.

**Core concept:** Cardiac troponin I/T rise 2-4h, peak 1-2d, persist 7-14d; hs-troponin detects earlier and excludes MI if negative at 3h.

**Key parameters:** Trop I not CKD (use T). T binds tropomyosin, I inhibitory. hs cutoff <14 ng/L; negative 3h excludes. MI = value >99th percentile + ischemia.

**Clinical application:** Serial hs-troponin rules in/out ACS rapidly. Trop T preferred renal failure.

**Interventions:** Repeat at 0/3h. Type 2: trend to confirm.

**Pitfalls:** Single normal early troponin misses event. CKD false rise with I. NSTEMI 30% initial ECG normal—rely on enzymes.

**Core concept:** ACS mainly from plaque rupture → collagen exposure → platelet adhesion/activation → GpIIb/IIIa conformational change → aggregation → occlusion. Vasospasm and O2 mismatch are secondary.

**Key parameters:** Three pathways: rupture/thrombus, coronary spasm (Prinzmetal, α1), supply-demand (tachy, fever, anemia, sepsis).

**Clinical application:** Drives antiplatelet (aspirin, P2Y12) and anticoagulation. Type 2 targets precipitant.

**Interventions:** Dual antiplatelet, statin, anticoag. Spasm: nitrates, CCB.

**Pitfalls:** Overlooking O2 mismatch in ICU (ventilated, septic) as Type 2. Silent MI in DM/transplant—no pain cue.

**Core concept:** ST elevation mimics include pericarditis, PE, spasm, Brugada, Takotsubo, SAH, hypothermia, intoxication; distinguish to avoid inappropriate PCI.

**Key parameters:** Pericarditis: concave, from S-not R, no reciprocal, multiterritory. PE: V1-3 ↑. Brugada: coved V1-3. Left main: ST↑≥2mm in ≥8 leads or aVR/V1.

**Clinical application:** Prevents futile cath; directs cause-specific therapy (PE anticoag, pericarditis NSAID).

**Interventions:** Left main/multivessel: urgent PCI. Mimics: treat underlying.

**Pitfalls:** Early tall T waves mimic hyperK. Propofol infusion syndrome causes ST↑ in ICU.

**Core concept:** 30% MI painless—silent ischemia in DM (autonomic neuropathy), ventilated ICU, elderly, post-transplant denervation.

**Key parameters:** Only 10% chest pain = MI; 33% arrest risk within 1h of acute pain. NSTEMI often normal ECG.

**Clinical application:** In ICU, screen troponin in ventilated/septic without pain. Transplant: no chest pain alarm.

**Interventions:** Low threshold serial troponin, ECG in high-risk silent groups.

**Pitfalls:** Attributing hemodynamic instability to sepsis alone; missed Type 2 MI. Analgosedation hides ischemia signs.
